rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Training platform = Claude Code (CLI + Desktop) + Cowork.
Verify capabilities BEFORE asserting, not after.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Maintainer block line 104 carries an explicit capability-check record confirmed 2026-04-29 by Antti (multi-session on shared dir, Cowork concurrent tasks, subagent spawn phrasing). Claims in student-facing body align with that record.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Known correct Claude Code facts (subagent dispatch, session semantics).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Subagent invocation pattern ('spawn three subagents in parallel') confirmed in maintainer block. Runtime-fork divs correctly applied for subagent/agent vocabulary split.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Sessions are the unit of work — write 'start a new session in X,' not 'open Claude Code in X.'
External org content — no import, reconciliation, or paraphrase.
Pseudo-URI + kill-switch for public/private content split.
No placeholders inside fenced blocks.
Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:48 — Debrief prompt preceded by a multi-sentence setup paragraph ('Five minutes. Claude reviews the session...') rather than a canonical one-sentence command-verb action lead-in. line:74 — Homework optional prompt preceded by bold label ('**Optional move: pressure-test the memory.**') rather than a command-verb one-sentence lead-in under 20 words.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a one-sentence command-verb action lead-in immediately before each **Prompt** label — e.g. 'Ask Claude to audit the session end-to-end and rewrite the rules file with specific seam evidence.' The existing setup paragraphs can stay but the canonical single-sentence lead-in should sit between them and the **Prompt** label.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Both prompts use '**Prompt** *(Claude Code)*' with blank line then fenced block. Shape is correct except for the action lead-in gap caught by rule 2.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Installed Claude Code Skills invoke by name, never by path.
Chain prompts by back-reference and deterministic path, never by invented alias.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Debrief prompt references deterministic paths (agents/, sources/, module-3/). Homework prompt references memory/ and sources/. Both well-anchored.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Markdown shout (**bold**, *italic*) inside fenced prompt blocks is dead weight in Claude Code.
Runtime-fork prompt blocks: paired divs, shared chip.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Runtime forks for subagent/agent vocabulary are applied correctly in LOs (line 13) and Key Concepts (lines 38, 41) via rt-code/rt-cowork spans. Open TODO in maintainer block (line 89) acknowledges remaining Cowork terminology gaps in unforked lines — below the <!-- maintainer --> boundary, not a student-facing violation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artifact destinations are explicit subpaths, never naked filenames (Bootstrap-specific).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Debrief prompt references module-3/ as directory, not naked filenames. ./CLAUDE.md uses the explicit ./ prefix per rule. Homework prompt references memory/ and sources/ as relative paths.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Source URL + type label — mandatory.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No practitioner citations or factual claims requiring source-type labels appear in student-facing body. Named frameworks (Rumelt, Martin, Sutherland) appear only in maintainer block plug-point notes, which are not student-facing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Zombie stat guard.
Forcing functions stay, scripted reactions go — and forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Debrief forcing function ('Audit harshly', 'Name at least TWO handoff seams with specific evidence') is inside the fenced prompt block. Post-prompt body (lines 59-61) explains the why — this is a post-prompt explanation carve-out, not a pre-prompt forcing function leak.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verification machinery needs a smoke-test before being trusted.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: This module does not ask the student to build verification machinery.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Context-loading tiers as staged progression.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Context-loading tier teaching is not in scope for this module.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artifact-realism check — named tools, skills, files must exist at this point.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: All referenced exercise files (personal-agent-homework.md, name-your-crux.md, three-retrievers-one-curator.md, three-minds-one-synthesis.md, module-3-prework.md), lecture files (agent-that-takes-action.md, when-to-split-an-agent.md, debugging-stuck-agents.md, module-4-prework.md), and supplementary files (what-is-an-agent.md, agent-trigger-list.md) verified present on disk.

platform_claims_checked.claim: parallel sessions on shared files (retrieval) and subagents inside one session (synthesis)

Two multi-agent shapes in Claude Code. Separate sessions on shared files (Phase 1: long-running, independent, visible). Subagents inside one session (Phase 2: quick parallel thinking, bounded return).

Multi-window / multi-session on the same working directory works on CLI (four terminal windows) and desktop (four sessions); 3 concurrent Cowork tasks on the same connected folder confirmed running 2026-04-29 (Antti); subagent launch phrasing ('spawn three subagents in parallel') confirmed working in current Claude Code.

citations_checked.claim: No factual claims with required citations appear in student-facing body. Named frameworks (Rumelt, Martin, Sutherland) appear only in maintainer block.
